Job Role Description: Functions of the Role This position is pivotal in improving the physical functioning and quality of life for our clients. The role involves assessing, treating, and rehabilitating clients with various age-related conditions, such as frailty, musculoskeletal issues, neurological impairments, and mobility limitations. The Physiotherapist collaborates with a multidisciplinary team to create person-centred care plans that promote independence, enhance mobility, and prevent functional decline. Roles and Responsibilities Organisation Works within the role and responsibilities for the position and complies with all organisational policies, regulations and standards, and the Code of Conduct; Maintains professional and technical knowledge by participating in training and development activities; Represents the organisation as the first point of contact via a range of communication methods; Contributes effectively to team effort by accomplishing related results as needed; Always maintains a high level of professionalism. Duties and Tasks Conduct comprehensive assessments of clients' physical function, including mobility, balance, strength, pain management, and posture; Managing a wide variety of client presentations across various funding areas including Support at Home Program (S@H), Commonwealth Home Support Program (CHSP), and NDIS; Identify risk factors for falls, immobility, and other physical challenges, and develop tailored physiotherapy care plans; Work with clients, their families and care team to set realistic rehabilitation goals that enhance independence and mobility; Ensure that care plans are regularly reviewed and updated to reflect changes in the clients' condition and/or goals; Assess and manage clients' pain, including implementing strategies for pain relief that optimise mobility and quality of life; Provide evidence-based physiotherapy treatments and interventions to address functional decline, improve mobility, and enhance quality of life; Implement strength, flexibility, and coordination exercises to improve clients' function and prevent the risk of falls; Assist with the rehabilitation of clients recovering from surgery, illness, or injury, including post-stroke, post-fracture, or post-surgical rehabilitation; Educate clients', their families and care team on appropriate exercises and strategies to maintain functional mobility and prevent future health issues; Conduct falls risk assessments and implement falls prevention strategies, including balance and strength training, education, and environmental modifications; Recommend and prescribe appropriate mobility aids (e.g., walkers, canes, wheelchairs) and train clients and team members in their use; Assess and adjust the home environment to enhance mobility and reduce falls risks; Provide manual handling training to team members. Other Ensures a strong customer service focus through responsive, effective customer engagement;Contributes effectively to organisational events and activities; Work closely with other health professionals (e.g., occupational therapists, nurses, doctors, dietitians) to ensure holistic care for clients;Actively participate in case conferences, multidisciplinary team meetings, and care reviews; Share expertise in physiotherapy with other team members through training and supervision, ensuring all team members understand the physiotherapy approach to care;Engage in ongoing professional development to stay up to date with best practice guidelines, treatment techniques, and regulatory changes in aged care.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Tertiary qualification in Physiotherapy; Current registration with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency (AHPRA); Ability to meet the requirements of Criminal History screening procedures; Current Drivers Licence; Current First Aid and CPR certificate; Experience working in an aged care setting, is highly desirable; Sound knowledge of aged care legislation, accreditation standards, and physiotherapy practices for older adults; Experience in assessing and managing conditions common in aged care, such as musculoskeletal issues, neurological disorders (e.g., stroke, Parkinson's), and post-surgical rehabilitation;Ability to develop and deliver individualised treatment plans based on evidence-based practices.

Our values are Integrity, Innovation, Collaboration and Respect.2. Job Role Description: About YouturnYouturn is a not-for-profit organisation established more than 30 years ago to address youth homelessness on the Sunshine Coast. Today it has evolved into a national organisation with a multidisciplinary team of professionals. Trusted to create safe, inclusive, and supportive communities, Youturn offers assistance to vulnerable people of all ages, during their most challenging times. At Youturn, our overarching goal is to make a lasting impact in the lives of those we serve. Through our dedicated efforts and partnerships, we aspire to build a society where everyone has the opportunity to lead a fulfilling and dignified life. Values Statement Supporting young and vulnerable people in their time of need, is at the heart of what we do at Youturn. Our organisational culture is built on trusting relationships that are meaningful and inclusive. We listen to understand, we embrace diversity and we offer a safe space free from judgement. To ensure our young and vulnerable people receive individualised support, we invest in team members who embody our values to foster a collaborative culture of excellence. The Administrative Officer role at Youturn is pivotal in providing comprehensive support across all operational aspects of the organisation. The incumbent will handle reception, secretarial, administrative (including MBS billing), communication, and organisational tasks in a youth-friendly environment.The primary goal is to offer reception and administrative assistance to all members of the centre, ensuring a high standard of care for young individuals and their families and friends. This position is vital to maintaining a professional and confidential atmosphere while supporting effective service delivery and day-to-day operations. The Administrator is crucial for ensuring that the service is perceived as accessible, welcoming and youth-friendly.Key Responsibilities Administration Perform various administrative tasks as required, including reception duties, telephone management, calendar maintenance, appointment management, database management, scanning, filing, and correspondence management. Provide general administration support including resource preparation, shopping, postage, mail in and out, and other general office administrative duties as required.  Administrative tasks associated with meetings including directed secretarial duties, scheduling, planning and preparation.  Administrative tasks as directed by the Line Manager to support the team with specific priorities including general daily housekeeping activities, facilities management including maintaining office/kitchen supplies, ensuring facilities are cleaned and well-equipped and organising regular and timely maintenance and repairs.  Provide administrative support for committees and sub-committees, including meeting preparation, minute-taking and post-meeting follow-up. Assist with the establishment and maintenance of office systems such as file management and cataloguing of resources and stationery. Organise bookings of meeting rooms, including arrangement of catering, equipment hire, key deposit and cleaning.  Manage office systems, file and brand management, and promotional materials. Ensure confidentiality of young people and Headspace information. Handle reception duties such as patient billing, medical history requests and appointment management. Ensure timely reminders for Mental Health Care Plans and Reviews. Perform other duties as directed that are within the limits of the employee’s skills, competence and training and the scope of the employee’s award classification.  General Duties Promote the program, communicate regularly with and build and maintain collaborative working relationships with program partners and all relevant stakeholders in the program. Maintain an open-minded and flexible approach to the work whilst maintaining the best practice principles contained within the Code of Conduct, policies and procedures of Youturn. Contribute to a positive representation of Youturn through supportive and positive working relationships with staff, young people, their families/carers/kin, stakeholders and the wider community. Participate in service staff meetings and professional development with the relevant Line Manager and other staff as appropriate. Participate in appropriate education to develop and maintain necessary knowledge and skills required to fulfil the responsibilities of the position. As an employee, work in a healthy and safe manner and encourage others to do the same; comply with all warning and safety signage; report or rectify any unsafe conditions; adhere to workplace health and safety policies and procedures. Perform other reasonable duties as directed, that are within the employee’s skill, competence, training and scope of the employee’s award classification. From time to time, Youturn may require you to work in areas that are not your normal work areas and perform functions that are outside of your usual duties.  Youturn may require you to do this so long as you have the skills and training to perform the functions that are outside of your normal work areas or usual duties.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Essential Experience, Qualifications & Other RequirementsEssentialTertiary qualifications and/or a minimum of 3 years' relevant experience in Office Administration/Reception in a medium-sized organisation, ideally in the Social Support environment or Medical Reception. Sound written and oral communication, interpersonal and customer service skills and the capacity to deal confidently and courteously with people at all levels. Strong organisational skills, the ability to successfully complete several tasks concurrently, maintain high levels of attention to detail, maintain documentation and record keeping, and meet deadlines. Current ‘C’ class Open Driver’s License. Knowledge and/or experience of MBS system and processes for patient billing or the ability to rapidly acquire relevant skills. Hold a current Working With Children Check (WWCC) issued by the relevant State or Territory. Example, Blue Card. Hold or be eligible to apply for, a Licensed Care Suitability Check issued by the Department of Child Safety, Youth Justice, and Multicultural Affairs. Desirable Certificate in a relevant discipline (Certificate IV Health Administration/Business Support) and/or substantial experience working in medical administration in a general practice or health sector. Selection Criteria Experience in an administration role with demonstrated understanding of the challenges, importance and issues of working in a dynamic environment. Demonstrated ability to work as part of a team, taking professional approach, acting in the best interest of Youturn and its clients, and being able to demonstrate Youturn Values.  Ability to tactfully respond to crisis situations and to handle information with confidentiality. Experience working with young people in a youth-friendly manner. Innovative problem-solving skills and ability to adapt to change. Understanding of the health and well-being of young people aged 12-25, particularly regarding mental health and substance use issues. Well developed proficiency, knowledge and experience in Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook and PowerPoint.  Strong organisational skills and attention to detail, with a demonstrated ability to manage competing workloads and rapidly changing priorities, including management and the ability to multi-task; prioritise, meet deadlines, and see tasks through to completion and on time.  Excellent interpersonal, written, and oral communication skills, in particular the ability to communicate with a broad cross section of people with a focus on communication with young people. Ability to work autonomously and as part of a team, with a willingness to take direction when given. Ability to adapt to a changing work environment. Experience in minute-taking or the ability to quickly acquire this skill.

Job Role Title: Assistant Manager - Whyalla and Central Eyre Peninsula
Job Role Description:
The Assistant Manager is responsible for providing: 
 Support to the Regional Manager with the general running of the regional office
 Operational line management according to the specific needs of the location and the programs delivered in that location 
 Maintaining a client case management component and/or deliver community education programs (where client services delivery forms part of the role)
 Provide regional program compliance across a specified program, or programs (as required)
Provide professional technical leadership/supervision to regional staff (when necessary)
Job Role Skills and Experience:
A degree or diploma in a human service and/or management related area
 Previous leadership experience, with ability to delegate and assign tasks
 Experience in providing client case management
 Effective team management, including staff coaching and development skills
 Strong written and verbal communication
 Demonstrated ability to practice a high level of confidentiality
 Demonstrated high level competency using Microsoft Office applications
 Ability to build and maintain effective working relationships and influence others through stakeholder collaboration and partnerships
 Proven capacity to work effectively within a team as well as the initiative to work unsupervised
 Strong organisational and time management skills with ability to work under pressure and manage conflicting priorities and multi-task
 Strong interpersonal skills with demonstrated experience working in cross-cultural environments 
 Previous experience in a leadership role in a social services organisation and/or ‘not for profit’ (NFP) organisation  (desirable)
 Knowledge of NFP social services programs in South Australia (desirable)
 Experience with file management including collating, maintaining, and reporting on accurate data and information (desirable)
 Report and tender writing skills (desirable)

Job Role Description: Maintains the work structure by updating job requirements and job descriptions for all positions. Maintains organisation staff by establishing a recruiting, testing, and interviewing program; counselling managers on candidate selection; conducting and analysing exit interviews; recommending changes. ​Prepares employees for assignments by establishing and conducting orientation and training programs.

Training and development expertise
	Project management skills
	Communication skills
	Leadership and interpersonal skills

Job Role Description: AWARD: Horticulture Award 2020CLASSIFICATION: Level 2POSITION PURPOSEThe purpose of the Farm & Garden Crew is to work with, supervise and assist a team of volunteers while planning, maintain and harvesting seasonal vegetable crops.The FareShare kitchen garden program spans three sites producing high-yield vegetable crops to ensure reliable access to fresh, healthy produce for our meals.KEY RESPONSIBILITIES    Market gardening (70%)•    Perform physical tasks required for garden maintenance, including:o    Planting, weeding, harvesting and post-harvest cleaning crops.o    Lifting and stacking heavy vegetable crates.•    Ensure work is performed in an organised fashion.•    Ensure equipment and location is left clean and tidy at the end of the shift.•    Operate and maintain irrigation pump equipment and perform irrigation-related maintenance.•    Use garden equipment/power tools safely to maximise productivityVolunteer supervision (30%)•    Coordinate and run garden volunteer shifts as directed by line manager.•    Guide and supervise volunteers in planting, maintaining, harvesting and cleaning activities.Risk and Safety •    Bring to the attention of the line manager any activity that has the potential to damage the reputation of SecondBite | FareShare or its partners, and/or lead to legal issues•    Demonstrate commitment to safety by performing duties in accordance with relevant legislation and SecondBite | FareShare policies, procedures and practices•    Immediately report and act on any accidents and hazardsOther Duties •    Maintain and contribute to a healthy work culture•    Provide accurate and timely information for the line manager and other reporting requirements•    Undertake regular one on one meetings with the line manager to ensure information/ knowledge is shared•    Undertake other duties within the employee’s skills and experience as directed by line manager
Job Role Skills and Experience: POSITION COMPETENCIESQualifications, Skills and Experience •    Formal qualifications in horticulture.•    Experience working in a market garden setting.•    Experience growing vegetables.•    Experience working with volunteers and/or in not-for-profit organisations or leading a team•    Current first aid certificate (or willingness to obtain one).Personal Attributes•    Demonstrated ability to work with a diverse team of people from different backgrounds and abilities.•    Proven ability to take direction and work independently.•    Physical fitness to perform the duties of the role, including standing, crouching, lifting, and working outdoors for extended periods.OTHER•    Regular Saturday shifts will be worked to fulfill the requirements of the causal role •    Ability to travel and work from multiple sites •    Out of hours work may be required occasionally to meet the requirements of the role

Job Role Description: The Administrator of Sunkids Children’s Centre will be involved in all aspects of the daily office duties of the centre.  The Administrator will act in the best interests of Sunkids and Approved Provider to ensure high quality customer service is provided and the centre is following legislative requirements and other standards as specified in the Policy Manual. The philosophy is used as the basis for all decision made.  Building and fostering supportive relationships will be the primary focus of the position.  The Administrator will be responsible for working in collaboration with the Centre Manager. The Administrator should have an understanding about all aspects of the centre’s operations to confidently secure new enrolments and converse with all families.
